title: Pouzolzia indica
taxonomy: Pouzolzia indica is classified within the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This plant is a part of the genus Pouzolzia, which is a group of flowering plants. Pouzolzia indica is native to certain regions of Asia, where it grows in various environments. As a member of the Urticaceae family, Pouzolzia indica is closely related to other nettle plants. The classification of Pouzolzia indica reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
uses: Pouzolzia indica is known to have several medicinal uses, including its application in treating various skin conditions. It is also used to reduce inflammation and fever. Pouzolzia indica is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat digestive issues and as a laxative. The plant has been found to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating infections. Additionally, Pouzolzia indica is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage.

side_effects: Pouzolzia indica is a plant species used in traditional medicine.
